Title: Elucidation of tropane alkaloid biosynthesis in Erythroxylum coca using a microbial pathway discovery platform
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces methylecgonone to methylecgonine and, with EcMPOBMT, converts a Solanaceae tropane pathway into one producing Erythroxylaceae-like CMO tropanes
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active tropane alkaloids; cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast pathway reconstruction; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; co-expression with EcMPOBMT and AbCYP82M3/AtATR1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key finishing enzyme for microbial production of methylecgonine and for pathway swapping between lineages
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcMecgoR reduces methylecgonone to methylecgonine. 2. Co-expression with EcMPOBMT redirects solanaceous yeast pathways toward Erythroxylaceae-like carbomethoxy tropanes. 3. It was required in the final reconstructed core coca pathway in yeast.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously characterized before this study, but experimentally central in the Results for pathway reconstruction.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Erythroxylum coca; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early polyamine branch leading to first tropane ring closure
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates spermidine at the internal N4 position using SAM to form N-methylspermidine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies committed intermediate for tropane alkaloid core formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active tropane alkaloids; cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, especially biosynthetically active young leaves and buds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of N-methylspermidine and supports the first committed step of Erythroxylaceae tropane alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; engineered yeast expression; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; qPCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: EcSPMT; EcAOF1; EcAOC1; EcAOC2; EcOGAS1; EcOGAS2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in buds, L1 and L2 leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in buds, L1 and L2 leaves of E. coca
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse-grown tissues; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering the committed entry step of Erythroxylaceae tropane alkaloid production in heterologous hosts
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcSMT catalyzes SAM-dependent spermidine N-methylation to N-methylspermidine. 2. Its expression is enriched in tropane alkaloid-producing coca tissues. 3. It supports the alternative Erythroxylaceae route to the first tropane ring via spermidine, distinct from Solanaceae PMT-mediated putrescine methylation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EcSMT showed SMT activity in vitro and in yeast, while EcSPMT showed host-dependent dual activity.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Erythroxylum coca; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early polyamine branch leading to first tropane ring closure
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acts as a spermidine synthase using dcSAM and, in planta/in vitro, also methylates spermidine using SAM to form N-methylspermidine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and committed intermediate for tropane alkaloid core formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active tropane alkaloids; cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves, especially buds and young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables production of spermidine and, in planta, N-methylspermidine to drive first ring closure in E. coca tropane alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified enzyme assays; engineered yeast expression; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; qPCR expression analysis
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: EcSMT; EcAOF1; EcAOC1; EcAOC2; EcOGAS1; EcOGAS2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in buds, L1 and L2 leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in buds, L1 and L2 leaves of E. coca
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Greenhouse-grown tissues; transient expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Host-dependent activity discrepancy: retains SPDS activity in yeast but loses SMT activity there
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for pathway engineering because one enzyme can provide both spermidine synthesis and N-methylation functions under suitable host contexts
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as committed-step enzyme in conjunction with EcSMT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcSPMT is bifunctional, catalyzing SPDS activity and, in vitro/in planta, SMT activity. 2. It co-expresses with known tropane alkaloid genes in coca tissues. 3. It supports the alternative spermidine-based entry route into tropane alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EcSPMT uniquely uses both SAM and dcSAM; SMT activity was not observed in yeast but was validated in planta.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana; Erythroxylum coca
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Flavin-dependent amine oxidase / polyamine oxidase family
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for N-methylpyrrolinium generation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active tropane alkaloids; cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Converts N-methylspermidine to N-methylputrescine, enabling the alternative Erythroxylaceae route to N-methylpyrrolinium
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Engineered yeast screening; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; fluorescence localization in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered yeast and transient plant expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical for reconstructing the coca-specific first ring closure route in microbial production systems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcAOF1 is the flavin-dependent oxidase that converts N-methylspermidine to N-methylputrescine. 2. It functions upstream of copper amine oxidases EcAOC1/2. 3. Its recruitment shows independent evolution of the Erythroxylaceae tropane pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Novel activity on a tertiary amine; cytosolic localization consistent with AOF family.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana; Erythroxylum coca
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes N-methylputrescine to the corresponding aldehyde, which cyclizes nonenzymatically to N-methylpyrrolinium
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to bicyclic core precursor N-methylpyrrolinium
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active tropane alkaloids; cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables N-methylputrescine oxidation and thereby supports N-methylpyrrolinium formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Engineered yeast screening with hygrine proxy; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; fluorescence localization in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered yeast and transient plant expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful alternative MPO component for synthetic reconstruction of coca alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcAOC1 oxidizes N-methylputrescine in the coca pathway. 2. It acts downstream of EcAOF1 and upstream of N-methylpyrrolinium-derived products. 3. It localizes to peroxisomes, consistent with copper amine oxidase family behavior.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EcAOC1 and EcAOC2 were both active; hygrine accumulation used as proxy for N-methylpyrrolinium formation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana; Erythroxylum coca
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes N-methylputrescine to the corresponding aldehyde, which cyclizes nonenzymatically to N-methylpyrrolinium
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to bicyclic core precursor N-methylpyrrolinium
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active tropane alkaloids; cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables N-methylputrescine oxidation and thereby supports N-methylpyrrolinium formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Engineered yeast screening with hygrine proxy; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; fluorescence localization in yeast; full pathway reconstruction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered yeast and transient plant expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Chosen in full reconstructed yeast pathway, making it a practical enzyme for synthetic biology deployment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcAOC2 is an active copper amine oxidase in the coca pathway. 2. It converts N-methylputrescine toward N-methylpyrrolinium formation. 3. It supported de novo methylecgonine reconstruction in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in final full pathway reconstruction strain CSY1343.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana; Erythroxylum coca
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two malonyl-CoA units to form 3-oxoglutarate used for MPOB formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for MPOB and downstream methylecgonine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active tropane alkaloids; cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides 3-oxoglutarate needed to condense with N-methylpyrrolinium-derived intermediate for MPOB formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Engineered yeast expression; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; final pathway reconstruction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered yeast and transient plant expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential precursor-supplying enzyme for heterologous production of coca tropane intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcOGAS1 supplies 3-oxoglutarate for MPOB formation. 2. It functions in reconstructed first-ring/second-step intermediate formation in plants and yeast. 3. It was used in full de novo methylecgonine reconstruction in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to E. novogranatense EnPKS1/2-related enzymes; 100% AA identity to reported orthologs was noted for EcOGAS1/2 collectively.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana; Erythroxylum coca
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses two malonyl-CoA units to form 3-oxoglutarate used for MPOB formation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for MPOB and downstream methylecgonine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active tropane alkaloids; cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Engineered yeast expression; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered yeast and transient plant expression
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative OGAS enzyme for pathway engineering and comparative reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcOGAS2 is an active 3-oxoglutarate-producing enzyme supporting the early coca pathway. 2. It functioned in transient reconstruction assays. 3. It supports the alternate Erythroxylaceae tropane scaffold-building route.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used functionally in plant reconstruction though final full yeast reconstruction used EcOGAS1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates the carboxyl group of MPOB to form the methyl ester MPMOB, enabling retention of the 2-carbomethoxy group
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor required for Erythroxylaceae-specific methylecgonone and methylecgonine branch
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: 2-carbomethoxy-containing tropane alkaloids including cocaine have dopamine transporter-binding properties
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables retention of the characteristic 2-carbomethoxy group in Erythroxylaceae tropane alkaloids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast candidate screening; LC-MS/MS detection of MPMOB; expression profiling; pathway conversion and hybrid alkaloid production in yeast; final pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Overexpressed in L1 leaves relative to L2 and L3 leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in L1 leaves of E. coca
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical engineering target to install the Erythroxylaceae-specific 2-carbomethoxy branch into heterologous or hybrid tropane pathways
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant / branch-defining step for CMO retention
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcMPOBMT is the missing SABATH methyltransferase that methylates MPOB. 2. It enables retention of the 2-carbomethoxy moiety specific to coca alkaloids. 3. Together with EcMecgoR it can redirect a Solanaceae pathway toward Erythroxylaceae-like CMO tropanes.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally identified as EcSABATH1 before functional renaming.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative cyclization of MPMOB to methylecgonone, forming the second tropane ring
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor of methylecgonine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Pharmacologically active tropane alkaloids; cocaine blocks monoamine reuptake
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the missing second ring closure to produce methylecgonone in the coca pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome co-expression clustering; yeast candidate screening; LC-MS/MS product validation; phylogenetic analysis; full pathway reconstruction in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified by co-expression with known tropane alkaloid genes across Erythroxylum transcriptomes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expression with known pathway genes in Erythroxylum transcriptomes
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential cyclase for installing methylecgonone formation and reconstructing the core coca pathway in microbes
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step / second ring closure determinant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcCYP81AN15 is the methylecgonone synthase of E. coca. 2. It converts MPMOB to methylecgonone and is unrelated to Solanaceae CYP82M3 tropinone synthase. 3. It enabled full de novo methylecgonine production when assembled with other core coca genes in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Initially screened as EcCYP9; PKC-like proteins were not required for its activity.
